original_hypothesis.title: Kernel can be irreversibly bricked by setting executor/admin to address(0), permanently halting security operations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Kernel.executeAction() supports Actions.ChangeExecutor and Actions.ChangeAdmin but does not validate target_ != address(0). If executor is set to zero, no one can call executeAction again, preventing module upgrades, policy activation/deactivation, or kernel migration. If admin is set to zero, no one can grant/revoke roles, preventing operational response (e.g., revoking CREATE_SIGNER) and policy admin actions that rely on roles.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational footgun that could be triggered accidentally or by a compromised executor. It directly blocks incident response and upgrade paths.
deployment_assumptions: Executor/admin are EOAs/multisigs that can make mistakes or be compromised.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Kernel.sol executeAction() assigns executor/admin directly with no zero-address guard.

proof_of_signal.lines: executeAction(): else if action_ == ChangeExecutor { executor = target_; } else if ChangeAdmin { admin = target_; }

preconditions: A ChangeExecutor/ChangeAdmin action is executed with target_ = address(0).

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Irreversible administrative halt possible via single transaction
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time executor can call executeAction

operational_questions: Is executor a multisig with strong transaction simulation to prevent zero-target assignments?
Are there monitoring alerts on ActionExecuted(ChangeExecutor/ChangeAdmin) to detect misconfiguration immediately?
validation_requirements: Simulate ChangeExecutor(0x0) and observe inability to upgrade/activate policies
Confirm no recovery mechanism exists (no backdoor) once executor=0

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call executeAction(ChangeExecutor, address(0)) and/or ChangeAdmin, address(0))
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Protocol becomes frozen operationally; no upgrades or role management possible

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Total loss of upgrade and governance control
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Cannot respond to incidents (e.g., revoke roles, upgrade modules)

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Sudden inability to executeAction or grantRole
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Executor change to zero or unexpected EOA

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
